2605.22678.title: Swift Sampling: Selecting Temporal Surprises via Taylor Series
2605.22678.paper_summary: While most frames in long-form video are redundant, the critical information resides in temporal surprises: moments where the actual visual features deviate from their predicted evolution. Inspired by the human brain's predictive coding, we introduce Swift Sampling, an elegant, training-free frame selection algorithm that automatically identifies high-information moments in a video. Specifically, we model a video as a differentiable trajectory in the visual latent space and compute the velocity and acceleration of its features. Then, we apply Taylor expansion to project the expected path of subsequent frames. Frames that diverge sharply from this predicted manifold are identified as temporally surprising frames and selected for sampling. Unlike prior training-free methods that rely on auxiliary networks or video-specific hyperparameter tuning, Swift Sampling is incredibly lightweight, adding only 0.02x additional computational cost over baseline making it 30x cheaper overhead than leading baselines. Across three long-video question answering benchmarks and 10 different downstream tasks, Swift Sampling outperforms uniform sampling and prior query-agnostic baselines. It is especially powerful for long videos with limited frame budgets improving accuracy by up to +12.5 points.
2605.22678.paper_summary_zh: 雖然長格式視頻中的大多數幀是冗餘的，但關鍵信息存在於時間上的驚喜中：實際視覺特徵偏離其預測演變的時刻。受到人類大腦預測編碼的啟發，我們介紹了Swift Sampling，一種優雅的無需訓練的幀選擇算法，能自動識別視頻中的高信息時刻。具體來說，我們將視頻建模為視覺潛在空間中的可微軌跡，並計算其特徵的速度和加速度。然後，我們應用泰勒展開來預測後續幀的預期路徑。與此預測流形明顯偏離的幀被識別為時間上驚訝的幀並被選擇進行取樣。與依賴輔助網絡或視頻特定超參數調整的先前無需訓練的方法不同，Swift Sampling 極其輕量，僅增加0.02倍的計算成本，相較於基準使其開銷便宜30倍。在三個長視頻問答基準和10個不同的下游任務中，Swift Sampling 超越了均勻取樣和先前的查詢無關基準。對於有限幀預算的長視頻，它特別強大，準確率提高了多達+12.5個點。
2605.22678.author: Dahye Kim et.al.
2605.22678.authors: Dahye Kim, Bhuvan Sachdeva, Karan Uppal, Naman Gupta, Vineeth N. Balasubramanian, Deepti Ghadiyaram
